one_line_ask: Build the recurring per-episode still brand kit for a daily-life vlog — color-grade the supplied frame-grab and shot stills to one consistent "personal, upbeat" look, produce a subject-pop YouTube thumbnail plus a 9:16 story-format still, vectorize the channel wordmark into clean lower-third and end-screen plates, gather it all on a Firefly board, and ship a same-length social reframe of one clip with a denoised voice-over.
brief:
You are the recurring post-production designer for "Princerrr," a daily-life vlog shot in 4K and delivered at 1080p. The video edit itself (trimming, jump-cuts, .srt captions, music, NLE project file) is handled elsewhere — your contract is the per-episode STILL BRAND KIT and the two media touches below, delivered consistently so every episode looks like the same channel.

Assets the client uploads for this episode: (1) a 4K frame-grab pulled from the hero clip (the creator at their desk, window light), (2) a separate DSLR "shot" still of the creator used for the thumbnail, (3) a flat PNG of the channel wordmark / logo lockup, (4) the hero clip itself (a 12s 4K horizontal segment), and (5) the raw lavalier voice-over scratch track (one-take, roomy).

What to do, as one chained job:
1. Normalize and color-grade BOTH stills to a single house look. Start from the frame-grab: auto-tone it, warm the white balance to match the creator's window-lit "personal, upbeat" feel, lift the shadows and tame the highlights, then push vibrance so it reads lively without going garish, and finally stamp a chosen Lightroom preset so the grade is repeatable across future episodes.
